title: S2 Works
wikitext:
[]: S2 Works             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Thu Mar 08, 2007 12:29 am I've owned all of the EVA music for awhile now, however one that I could never get a hold of was S2 Works (I could, just it would be too long of a process). I just today finally found a new active means of obtaining it. All I've really got to say is that you can kind of tell Sagisu started doing some music and was expecting a "normal" series and Anno stepped in, slapping down some classical music tapes and explaining what he needed. That, or he just underestimated the series darker half. As there are some songs on here that I could never see being used in EVA, even in the most slap stick comedy parts that no person in their right mind would even consider having a relation with EVA.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 08, 2007 3:02 pm I was able to get it via the magic of the internet. Only problem is each disc is one long .mp3 track, which gets kinda annoying having to skip through the whole thing to get to a piece you really like. There are a LOT of variations of different pieces, regardless of weather or not they where used in Series. I hope Theme of Love (though it's incomplete), Anxiety Ridden Honeymoon ~Mellow Version~ (either the nonpiano one or one of the piano variations) and the Asuka's Death piece are used in Rebuild, those are really nice. There were a few others I liked, I just can't remember what they were. Though I'm glad Anno didn't go ahead and use what would have been the Eyecatch audio. (the music isn't so bad, but the narration's ("Neon Genesis Evangelion") weird. Espeically the female version with the lady singing "Evangelion". I like the male version better, but still... No, just. No. ) Would you want me to PM you a track list I found online so you can get an idea of what's on what disc?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Mar 08, 2007 3:33 pm About half the tracks on the S2 works are good-very good, and the other half are Zzzzzz

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Thu Mar 08, 2007 3:58 pm BrikHaus81 wrote: About half the tracks on the S2 works are good-very good, and the other half are Zzzzzz

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 08, 2007 8:30 pm TriLink12 wrote: A good of example of "Zzzzzz" is that guy on Dics 6 (I think) saying "Neon Genesis Evangelion" over and over in not very good english. I never understood why they put stuff like that on there.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Thu Mar 08, 2007 10:06 pm the same bad download spot?

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Mar 08, 2007 10:49 pm Sorry guys, I feel the need to play CDs through my kickass audio system (don't ask how much I paid to get its kickasseriness), so I actually went to eBay and paid $30, including shipping, for a new/sealed version. Just arrived a few days ago. Haven't heard it yet, but I will probably listen to it this weekend.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 08, 2007 11:04 pm $30? I smell Son May.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Mar 08, 2007 11:13 pm Son May makes some nice bootlegs. I'm not joking, the quality is surprisingly good.

[]:             Quote Post by C4 » Thu Mar 08, 2007 11:15 pm The S2 Works is by far the best soundtrack collection I own. The only thing I could have done without, though I am still glad that it's on there, is the multiple versions of "A Moment When Tension Breaks". I myself love the eyecatch themes, if only to see other directions that the show could have taken musically. If you're a fan of Eva music, it's a no-brainer to buy this; it has every original BGM from the series, and any one of those tracks to me is dynamite. There's just a couple of things you can only find on S2 that make it worth the price for me, first of which is the regular version of M-9.(A different version of "Enlargement of Blockade", or "Heisoku no Kakudai" for you roomaji fans.) Up until "Refrain of Evangelion", it was the only place you could hear "Everything You've Ever Dreamed," which is an amazing song. It's also the only place to hear: 1. The studio version of the choral arrangement of "Borderline Case" that was heard during the "Evangelion Ethnic" sectoin of the live concert. 2. M-14, which is a badass 70's detective TV series to me. 3. The latter half of Disc 2 is all different unused(for the most part) emotional themes, all of which are great. But yeah, it's a must have if you're an Eva music fan, with the live concert being a somewhat far second.(I'd put Evangelion VOX before that, but some people don't like R&B, and the rap tracks on that album vary from pretty weak to somewhat interesting.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 08, 2007 11:24 pm C4 wrote: If you're a fan of Eva music, it's a no-brainer to buy this

[]:             Quote Post by C4 » Thu Mar 08, 2007 11:30 pm Reichu wrote: But if you're going to bother buying it, buy a real copy. With the unique ID number stamped on the box and everything.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 08, 2007 11:38 pm C4 wrote: Oh, yeah, no doubt. I wholeheartedly support getting all of your Evangelion music imported first, before downloaded.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Mar 09, 2007 3:09 pm Reichu wrote: Sweet Jebus, I wish that dodgy translation of mine would just die. Where did you get it from?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 09, 2007 3:31 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: What I find curious about S2 Works is how we get the strings version of JESU... but not the piano version.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Mar 09, 2007 9:03 pm Reichu wrote: $30? I smell Son May.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Fri Mar 09, 2007 10:52 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Reichu wrote: $30? I smell Son May.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 09, 2007 11:04 pm TriLink12 wrote: I thought I had the real deal too, but after listening to you guys talk I'm not so sure.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Fri Mar 09, 2007 11:20 pm Aww crap...It's fake. I always thought it looked a little off, (too plain looking if that makes sense), but never thought anything of it. Oh well... As long as I get the music I want then I don't much care.

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Fri Mar 09, 2007 11:26 pm All of my EVA soundtracks are .FLAC files, which are huge and lossless. I'm rather sure it's around 12GB for all of them. Worth it though, play them are any volume and they sound great, that's what 1050+kps will do though (compared to the standard 128/192kps). I don't see how anyone can stand "VOX" I would delete it, but then I really wouldn't have a complete collection. The songs are a mockery to some of the brilliant music used in EVA, to me at least. There's some good songs on S^2 Works, but there's also a lot of crappy ones. So the 1:1 thing pretty much is true for it. Does it really matter if you have a legit copy? You got them cheaper, they sound as good, look at good, are as good. Does Gainax really need any more money?
